speaker: LYDIA LU - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: call.

text: On the call with me are Jyotai Chairman, Adi Wu, Chief Executive Officer, Toby Xu, Chief

text: and uncertainties.

text: earnings press release and investor presentation.

text: Our comments will be on year-over-year comparisons unless we state otherwise.
speaker: LYDIA LU - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: And with that, let me turn the call over to Adi.
speaker: LYDIA LU - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Go ahead, Josef and Ryo.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Welcome to Alibaba Group's fiscal year 2026 fourth quarter earnings call.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Over the past quarter, Alibaba's high-intensity investment in our two strategic priorities
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: of AI plus cloud and consumption is rapidly translating into tangible business results.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: With Group revenue growing 11% year-over-year.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: This quarter, cloud intelligence groups external revenue growth accelerated to 40% and AI-related
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: product revenue achieved triple-digit growth for the 11th consecutive quarter.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: China E-commerce CMR grew 8% year-over-year on a like-for-like basis and the Quick Commerce
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: market achieved significant unit economics improvement while maintaining market share.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: We are at a pivotal inflection point in the evolution from conversational chatbots
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: to autonomous AI agents, which is directly driving explosive growth across our across
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: three core workload categories, training inference and agent orchestration.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Against this backdrop, Alibaba's AI has moved beyond the initial investment phase and progressed
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: commercialization at scale.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Next let me walk you through four areas in detail.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: The AI commercialization, cloud infrastructure, the AI application ecosystem and our consumption
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: business.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: First, the AI and cloud commercialization inflection point has arrived.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: This quarter, cloud intelligence groups annualized AI-related product revenue has surpassed 35.8
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: billion RMB continuing to maintain triple-digit growth.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: AI-related product revenue now accounts for 30% of cloud intelligence groups external
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: revenue.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: As we expect in about one year, AI-related product revenue will cross the 50% threshold
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: becoming the primary engine driving the cloud business's revenue growth.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: As a result, cloud intelligence groups external revenue growth is expected to continue accelerating
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: beyond its current 40% rate over the coming quarters.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Given the certainty of long-term AI demand and our full-stack technology advantages,
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: we expect this trajectory to sustain strong growth over the medium to long-term.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: This reflects AI's role in driving a comprehensive upgrade of Alibaba Cloud's entire business
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: as its growth engine fully pivots from traditional compute and storage to models AI-compute
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: and agent services.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: We're also seeing exponential growth in AI model and application services revenue.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: A new revenue engine driven jointly by foundation model services and AI-native software.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: This past three months, token consumption volumes on our model services platform grew
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: substantially quarter over quarter as enterprise customers accelerated their shift from simple
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: tasks to production scale and complex workloads, driving continued growth and demand for model
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: and application services on the model studio platform.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: We expect model and application services annualized recurring revenue, ARR, inclusive of the model
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: studio platform to surpass 10 billion RMB in the June quarter and 30 billion RMB by year
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: end.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: The harm margin profile of this revenue stream is becoming increasingly apparent making it
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: a source of healthy high quality growth.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Second, our AI infrastructure underpins our full technology stack and constitutes a durable
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: quote.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: T-heads proprietary GPU chips achieved scaled mass production with over 60% of compute
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: capacity already serving external customers across internet, financial services and autonomous
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: driving verticals.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: As the only AI cloud provider in China capable of delivering self-developed AI chips at scale,
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: we've secured autonomy over our compute supply chain while providing customers with highly
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: competitive AI inference and training services.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: In an environment of compute scarcity, this structural advantage is favorable to our revenue
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: growth and gross margin improvement.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: At the same time, our cloud products are accelerating their AI oriented upgrade.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: The surge in agent workloads has significantly elevated demand for traditional cloud products
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: built around CPU storage and containers and we're upgrading these into infrastructure
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: solutions optimized for the agent era.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Third, at the application layer, we've built a complete closed loop spanning
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: AI native software to a full agent ecosystem.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Alibaba token hub, ATH continues to launch new products connecting consumer and enterprise
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: environments with breakthrough progress in AI native software and coding agents.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: The QN model continues to iterate across reasoning, coding and agent capabilities.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: On the enterprise side, we've launched a range of products spanning intelligent workplace
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: tools, AI coding and business operations management, helping enterprises unlock creator
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: productivity.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: On the consumer side, the QN app fully integrated Taobao and T-Malls commerce service capabilities
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: on May the 7th.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: With this QN app is now deeply embedded across the ecosystem, spanning Taobao, Alipay, AMAP
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: and Fliggy, making it China's first all-in-one personal assistant to seamlessly bridge everyday
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: life productivity and learning.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Fourth, across our consumption business and at the group level, we're prioritizing long-term
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: value.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Beyond AI, our consumption strategy continues to progress steadily with CMR growth rebounding
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: significantly.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: This quarter, CMR grew 8% year over year on a like-for-like basis as we continue to improve
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: user experience and merchant operating efficiency.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: The quick commerce business achieved significant unit economics improvement while maintaining
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: stable market share, that market scale.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: In summary, the return on our investments in AI plus cloud and consumption are increasingly
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: clear.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: AI plus cloud revenue growth is accelerating with improving margins, model and application
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: services, ARR continues to grow at pace and operating efficiency across our consumption
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: business continues to improve.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Facing the historical opportunity that AI represents Alibaba is a pivotal juncture where our technology
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: investments are beginning to pay off commercially.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: We'll maintain our strategic resolve and leverage our full stack AI capabilities to support
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: long-term growth.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: That concludes my prepared remarks.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Next, I'll hand over to Toby to walk you through our financial results.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Thank you.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Thank you, Eddie.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Our strategic priorities remain laser focused on AI plus cloud and consumption businesses.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Multiple growth catalysts, including technological advancement and business innovation, are
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: aligning to create strong tailwinds.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: On AI plus cloud, our full stack capabilities spend models, cloud infrastructure and applications
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: with established leadership in every layer, the strong growth of our AI plus cloud businesses
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: and the clear path to monetization of our mass platform give us confidence to make significant
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: investments to extend our leadership.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: On consumption, we achieved strong CMR growth on a like-for-like basis during the quarter
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: and our quick commerce business continued to improve UE and AOV quarter over quarter.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Now let's look at the financial results for this quarter.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: On a consolidated basis, total revenue was R&B 243.4 billion, excluding revenue from
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Sunnah and Inan time, revenue on like-for-like basis will have grown by 11%.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Total adjusted in the beta decreased 84% primarily due to our strategic investments
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: in technology businesses, quick commerce and user experience, partly offset by the
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: improved operating results supported by continued growth in consumer management service and
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: cloud business and enhanced operating efficiencies across various businesses.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Our gap net income was R&B 23.5 billion and increase of 96% primarily attributable to
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: the year-over-year increase in net gain from mark-to-mark changes of our equity investments
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: and disposal losses of Sunnah and Inan time in the same quarter last year, partly offset
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: by the decrease in adjusted in beta.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: An increase in cash flow was an inflow of R&B 9.4 billion.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Free cash flow was an outflow of R&B 17.3 billion.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: We are reinvesting our operating cash flow to enhance our competitive advantage in AI.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: As of March 31, 2026, we held approximately US$38 billion in net cash, excluding debt
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: with maturity beyond five years.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Our net cash position stands at approximately US$59 billion.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: This balance sheet strength gives us confidence to invest for growth.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Now let's look at our consumption businesses.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: New from China e-commerce group was R&B 122 billion and increase of 6%.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Customer management revenue increased by 1% to help merchants grow their businesses and
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: increase willingness to spend our platform.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: We upgraded our business development program for select merchants during the quarter, under
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: which the level of platform subsidies for these merchants is directly tied to their
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: marketing span on our platform.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: For accounting purposes, such subsidies previously recorded as sales and marketing expenses
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: are now recorded as a contract revenue item to CMR.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Accordingly, CMR grow 1% year over year during the quarter, excluding the contract revenue
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: in half from the program on a like-for-like basis, CMR would have grown 8% year over year.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Revenue from our quick commerce business increased 57% to R&B 20 billion.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: The quick commerce business further improved the UE and increased the AOV quarter over
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: quarter, primarily driven by order mix optimization.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Alibaba China e-commerce group adjusted a beta was R&B 24 billion, a decrease of 40%
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: primarily due to the investment in quick commerce, user experience and technology, while there
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: is positive contribution from customer management service.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Excluding loss from our quick commerce business, our Alibaba China e-commerce group beta
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: would have been stable year over year and will fluctuate quarter over quarter due to significant
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: investment in merchant retention and user experience.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Revenue from AIDC grew 6% this quarter.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: AIDC's adjusted beta loss narrowed significantly year over year, approaching break even, driven
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: by a combination of logistics optimization and operating efficiency.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: The uni-gromics of our express choice business continue to improve substantially on sequential
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: basis.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Next, let's look at the business updates and results of cloud intelligence group.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Our cloud business delivered another quarter of accelerating growth.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Revenue from external customers accelerated to growth 40%.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: AI related products continue to lead this momentum.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: We delivered our 11th consecutive quarter of triple digit growth in AI revenue.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Its share of external cloud revenue continue to increase, now account for 30%.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: This quarter's AI revenue is R&B 9 billion and the annual revenue run rate is on the
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: 36 billion or US dollar 5.3 billion.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: This is a clear reflection of the scale and acceleration in our AI business.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: The adjusted beta margin remain relatively stable at 9.1%.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: All other segment revenue decreased by 21% to R&B 65.5 billion, mainly due to the disposal
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: of sunnah and in-time businesses, as well as the decrease in revenue from Tanya, partly
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: offset by the increase in revenue from Freshipo and AMAC.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: All others, adjusted beta was a loss of R&B 21.2 billion, primarily due to the increase
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: the investment in technology businesses, including foundation models and the consumer facing
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Queen APP.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: As we close this fiscal year, we remain committed to delivering consistent shareholder returns.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: Our board of directors has approved an annual dividend of US dollar 1.05 per ADS.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: We will continue to invest decisively in AI and consumption businesses, where we seek
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: significant long-term growth potential in our competitive advantages of compounding.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: We believe these investments to deliver growth and returns over time, ultimately creating
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: greater value for our shareholders.
speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you, Joe, Eddie, Toby, Fan and Lydia.

text: target for the first time.
speaker: RONALD KEUNG - ANALYST, GOLDMAN SACHS

speaker: RONALD KEUNG - ANALYST, GOLDMAN SACHS

text: third-party models.

speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: application service revenue.

text: That really comprises mainly two things.

text: On the one hand, it includes revenue from API calls on mass on our Bilean platform.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: It also includes revenues from our AI software subscriptions.

text: At present, most of the revenue is coming from the first of those two pieces, but this is
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: an open platform.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: So we are providing access both to our proprietary models as well as third-party models, including

text: open-source models and closed models.

text: including QAN as well as Tmoar as well as our voice and video generating models.

text: And then we will be able to make sure that the data is available.

text: that they need to do a lot more inferencing than in the past.

text: for higher prices and we have increased per token prices is good and the demand continues

text: to be high and growing.
speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

text: In fact, our ability to supply this demand is not able to keep up with all the growth
speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

text: and demand.
speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

text: We actually have a lot of customers still waiting to access the service.
speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

text: Inherently, mass will have higher growth margin than I ask.

text: That's important to know.

speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: advance.

speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

speaker: TOBY XU - CFO

text: net cash flow.

speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

text: business and that cash flow can in terms be used to support the development of the relevant
speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

text: infrastructure.

text: An additional point is that we have a very strong balance sheet.

text: As of March 31, 2026, we held approximately 38 billion US dollars in net cash.
speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

text: And if you exclude debt with maturity, beyond five years, our net cash position stands at
speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

text: approximately 58 billion US dollars.

text: So that balance sheet strength also gives us confidence to reinvest for growth.
speaker: JOE TSAI - CHAIRMAN

text: And beyond that, I would also add that we have a very strong capacity for pursuing

text: financing in capital markets.

text: development.

speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Well, first, as a result of our strong investments at QuickCommerce, we've achieved very rapid

text: position.

text: Compared to the same quarter last year, which was, of course, prior to all this large-scale

text: investment, both our order volume and our market share have increased significantly.
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: Overall order volume was 2.7 times that of the same quarter last year with non-food orders

text: at three times.
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: improvement in UE through enhanced fulfillment logistics efficiency as well as order mix
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: optimization.
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: So we are confident that UE will turn positive by the end of fiscal year 27.
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: While optimizing UE, we will continue to innovate to improve the experience for both consumers
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: and merchants, thereby sustaining our long-term competitiveness in QuickCommerce.
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: We're confident that our QuickCommerce business will achieve overall profitability in the
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: future at new scale and market share.
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: business as demonstrated in driving customer acquisition, enhancing user engagement, fulfilling
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: infrastructure.
speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

speaker: JIANG FAN - CEO, ALIBABA E-COMMERCE BUSINESS GROUP

text: I think that's a good question.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: I think fundamentally from the perspective of AI development, it's really all about paradigm
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: shift in computing and it's about leveraging this new technology to help users, whoever
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: they are, to complete tasks and solve problems.

text: And that applies equally on the 2C side as well as on the 2B side.

text: Now certainly at present we see higher willingness to pay on the 2B side because it's easier to
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: show a business case with compelling ROI for a business.

text: And at present most of our infrastructure resources are therefore channeled to the 2B side.

text: But at the end of the day, AI ultimately is an invention that's there to be a helper

text: and assisted to humans, to help humans with a whole range of things spanning their own
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Thanks, management. I have a follow-up question also on the future growth of the cloud business.
speaker: JOYCE JU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: And I'd just like to understand what your view is on EBITDA margins in the cloud business over the next few quarters.
speaker: JOYCE JU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: Do you think as this business accelerates, we can expect to see similar margins as we see in your international peers?
speaker: JOYCE JU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: I think when it comes to the deep penetration of AI technology across all different industries, we're still really in the early days of that long process.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: But our objective is clear. Our objective is to achieve growth to drive growth, to drive growth in token consumption, and to acquire a larger market share.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: We aim to maintain growth that is faster than the market average in order to gain larger market share and firmly cement our absolute market leadership position.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: So those are the primary objectives, and margin is still secondary.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: The other thing to be pointed out is that for the next three or even five years to come, there are physical constraints on production,
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: production capacity for chips, for memory, for the physical things that are needed to support all this growth in demand.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: And the advantage that we have at Alibaba Cloud is the scale of our customer base as well as the scale effect from all of the CAPEX that we've put in over these years.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: But in this environment of market scarcity, we're already seeing that the cost for us to deploy one new server this year is double what that same server would have cost a year ago.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: The cost inflation has been over 100%. So given that higher replacement cost effect, we have certain pricing power with respect to new customers and also old customers.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: So I think in the long term, the asset pricing effect will be positive for our revenues going forward.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: Secondly, we see very rapid growth in MAS as we reported to you, and inherently as we said, MAS represents a much higher level of gross margin than IAS or traditional types of IT operations.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: So as demand for inference continues to grow exponentially, we expect this will be very positive for growth margin.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: And due to the optimization of our reasoning technology, the output capacity, the productivity of a single cart will continue to rise.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: An additional factor is as we continue to scale up the deployment of the T-head, the T-head chips represent the highest value for money compute power on the cloud platform, and that also will contribute to a better gross margin.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO

text: But for several objective reasons that I've outlined, I think overall in the next two to three years, we can expect to see significantly higher gross margin for Alibaba Cloud, and we can expect to start to see that in the next one to two quarters.
speaker: EDDIE WU - CEO
